title: The Forest
notes:
The camera keeps moving down when using keyboard and mouse, it is possible to mitigate the issue by pressing the windows key and selecting the game window again, but that unpins the mouse cursor from the center of the screen, limiting the camera movement.
The game works flawlessly using a controller.

The game crashes upon quitting.

title: The Forest
notes: Flawless

Mouse doesn't really work (going out of the game in back in again does NOT work too), but with a gamepad it is fine
Crashes on exit and doesn't start again until you reboot. You must use the command "killall -KILL TheForest.exe" to close The Forest
title: The Forest

notes: hangs on exit (needs to be killed manually), With Ryzen 7 CPU taskset -c 0,2,6,8,10,12 helps a lot, 40 vs 60 FPS, somehow with all cores enabled it nearly consumes all of them but performance is pretty bad then.
title: The Forest

notes: no problems after 1h
title: The Forest

notes: Hangs on exit, procces must be killed. After some days playing multiplayer with friends, no errors at all.
title: The Forest

notes: Hangs on exit. Procces must be killed. No errors at all after 2 weeks playing multiplayer with friends.
title: The Forest

notes: performance great but when moving the mouse right the view also goes down and when up the view moves also right and controller doesn't work in the menus otherwise with a controller completely playable
title: The Forest

notes: Just after adding Xinput via winetricks and enabling it in the ingame settings, mouse works without errors. before it felt wrong
title: The Forest

notes: Starts directly out of the box. I only experienced some issues with semi-transparent trees. Also, after exiting the game, the game crashes. But besides that: Playable smoothly.
title: The Forest

notes: Mouse glitches to bottom right (Unity wide problem) - VR doesn't start at all. Otherwise seemingly playable
title: The Forest

notes:
Had to install xinput from winetricks and tweak some settings in game to make the mouse to kinda work.
Also had to run at lower graphics than i normaly would in Windows.
Sometimes i get very small lagspikes.
The game crashes when it exits too.
title: The Forest

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX Vega (VEGA10, DRM 3.23.0, 4.15.0-33-generic, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes:
No graphics glitches, but poor framerate (under 60 fps at all lowest 1080p, 36-40 fps at 1440p maxed)

Has a mouse bug, where your view slowly drifts to the lower right when doing any mouse action, including clicks and scrolling. This gets fixed by alt+entering twice, but then the pointer doesn't lock to the game, causing it to go out of the game window on multi-monitor setups.
title: The Forest

notes: hangs on exit (needs to be killed manually), With Ryzen 7 CPU taskset -c 0,2,6,8 to make it perform good. Somehow with all cores enabled it nearly consumes all of them but performance is pretty bad then. Mouse drifts a bit onto the bottom of the screen when turning. Mouse in crafting book drifts down even more. 2560x1440, allmost maxed, motion blur off, film grain off, FPS between 45-90. Mostly around 70
title: The Forest

notes: mouse glitch in desktop mode still there - VR now works, options menu cant be adjusted (I think this bug is not related to linux, it doesn't work on windows aswell) and can't exit options menu - game has to be killed. otherwise performance seems pretty bad, but that could be due to my hardare and not having the ability to change the graphics settings.
title: The Forest

notes: Installed, Started right up and played with friends!
title: The Forest

notes: Everything works, and the performance is half decent too. The only issue is that whenever you move the mouse horizontally, it will try to go slightly downwards to the right as well (most noticable with subtle movements, like aiming), the cursor also moves when you use mouse clicks. This issue does not affect Lutris, so installing the game via lutris is currently the best workaround.
title: The Forest

notes: Runs good. To fix the mouse issue, you need to install xinput with winetricks and start the game. You must press Alt+Enter twice. After that the mouse works great. Or you can use a gamepad (I use a Xbox 360 controller).
title: The Forest

notes: I must now apply a registry file to edit the game options... this is not proton related! @Alexander/CrackedCrafterz
title: The Forest

notes: I was able to play the game with my friends out of the box, no fixes or tweaks required. Performance is pretty poor though. Average frame rate was somewhere between 25 and 35 fps regardless of resolution, up to 4k; so probably a CPU bottleneck. Pinning the game to physical cores using taskset didn't help.
title: The Forest

notes:
I don't know what exactly changed, the game was working mostly fine (as I explained in a report below) but all of a sudden, without the version of the game or proton changing, the game is now barely playable. Settings can no longer be configured (defaulting to High settings @ 640x480 resolution... if I try to change it, the game ignores any changes I make to the settings) and some text is rendered incorrectly. The lutris version also no longer launches properly (again just suddenly) . But if I launch the exe with my system's installed wine-staging, the game plays just fine without a hassle, however I cannot get it to launch through steam that way, so in other words, you can play single player without achievements if you play with your own wine installation (or configure lutris to run it without steam) or you can play it with horrible settings on proton right now.

Hopefully this will get fixed in the next proton release/beta.
title: The Forest

notes:
The currently walkaround for video settings issue is now to download [this](http://ul.to/2gt141nv) registry file and import them over the wine registry.

The Registry can be opened with an example like this WINEPREFIX='/home/alexander/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/242760/pfx' regedit

Please dont change the preset in the game launcher or the settings gets lost.
title: The Forest

notes: The new link for the registry file http://ul.to/f/x65rhk sry guys :D
title: The Forest

notes: Well it always says that it crashed after closing and steam thinks that there is always an update but it works perfectly with good performance.
title: The Forest

notes: Game runs fine, occasionally crashes. The issues are worse with multiplayer, but altogether performs very well.
title: The Forest

notes: Runs good on high settings with an average FPS of 60 - 80 in Single player and 50 - 60 in Multiplayer. No issues so far, except the mouse, which behaves sometimes strangely, but it's not too annoyingly. Definitely playable.
title: The Forest

notes: Start the game wtih "unset LC_ALL && %command%" and all save issues are gone! @Alexander
